title: enhance error message when remove a container does not exist
body:
`error reading build container: container not known` shows twice in the error message.

```
# buildah rm busybox-working-container-4
error reading build container "busybox-working-container-4": error reading build container: container not known
error reading build container: container not known
```

comments.body: @gouyang Just to clarify, the busybox-working-container-4 did not exist when you tried to remove it?  Thanks for the reports, you're keeping us on our toes and I like it!
@TomSweeneyRedHat yes, the busybox-working-container-4 did not exist.

My guess is since multiple containers can be given to `buildah-rm` if it fails to remove them it prints this message on the screen and in the end returns the error. Thus, the duplicate error message.

It shouldn't take much to fix it.
We handle this very differently in libpod.  Basically code returns lastError, if an Error happens when lastError is set, lastError is printed out , and then replaced with the new lastError.
Thanks @rhatdan, let me change that to libpod's way.

Closing as PR #458 fixes this problem.
Feel free to reopen if it wasn't fully fixed. Thanks.
